_comment: Task 1 measurement of the REAL gc-role-worker fragment. Operator ruling: MEASURE gc, do not infer it. These are OUR derived facts only; the fragment's copyrighted source is NEVER committed (compat spec §10). Re-derived by ci/gc-compat/worker_contract.py on every GCPACKS_REF move (Task 11 Step 4.5); drift fails the gate.
fragment_note: A Go-template ({{ define "gc-role-worker" }} ... {{- end }}) of 239 lines; identical bytes at gascity/template-fragments/gc-role-worker.template.md and gascity/roles/prompts/shared/gc-role-worker.md.tmpl. The runnable core is the 140-line GC_CLAIM bash heredoc (fragment lines 32-171). The rest is prose the LLM follows: set gc.outcome metadata, close the bead, continue (re-hook) or drain.

verbs_static._comment: Step 3 — EXHAUSTIVE-by-construction over the whole fragment TEXT (grep -oE '\b(gc|bd) [a-z][a-z-]*' | sort -u), independent of which branch runs. Ground truth for reconciliation (Step 4).

verbs_static_superset_ok._comment: Fold-in NB1: verbs_static MUST be a superset of every per-branch dynamic verb, so an under-capturing static grep cannot silently shrink verbs_static and pass reconciliation. Verified: the dynamic verb-pairs {gc hook, gc runtime, bd show, bd update, bd close} are all present in verbs_static.pairs.

hook_json_fields._comment: Fields the fragment's inline python3 json_pick reads out of `gc hook --claim --json`. json_pick reads the FIRST element if the response is a JSON array. On drain the fragment keys on `action` before consulting the exit code.

bd_show_json_fields._comment: Fields the fragment reads out of `bd show <id> --json`. `assignee` OVERWRITES the hook's assignee (fragment lines 128-130); `metadata.gc.routed_to` OVERWRITES the hook's route (lines 131-134). These overwritten values are the ones the fragment compares.

hook_work.why: fragment line 101 rejects (CLAIM_REJECTED; sleep 2; continue) unless CLAIM_CODE==0 for a work action

hook_drain.why: fragment line 95 keys on JSON action=="drain" BEFORE checking CLAIM_CODE; the drain exit code is never consulted. Camp uses ShimExit(1) on drain (spec/plan), which is compatible — the fragment ignores it and reads `action`.

runtime_drain_ack.why: the fragment runs `gc runtime drain-ack` then `exit 0` on both the CONFIG_REJECTED and NO_ROUTED_WORK paths; its exit code is not consulted.

config_rejected.why: missing assignee OR missing python3 -> echo CONFIG_REJECTED; gc runtime drain-ack; exit 0 (fragment lines 38-48)

no_routed_work.why: drain action -> echo NO_ROUTED_WORK; gc runtime drain-ack; exit 0 (fragment lines 95-99)

env_read.note: EXPECTED_ASSIGNEE = first non-empty of the assignee chain (fragment line 35). EXPECTED_ROUTE = first non-empty of the route chain (line 36). The fragment also EXPORTS (writes, does not read) GC_BEAD_ID, GC_ROOT_BEAD_ID, GC_CONTINUATION_GROUP.

comparison_invariants._comment: The load-bearing equalities the fragment enforces after a claim. If any fails, the fragment prints CLAIM_REJECTED and loops `sleep 2; continue` FOREVER (a hang) — this is why the §14 gate's env must match the cooked bead row.
comparison_invariants.assignee: bd show `assignee` (== camp claimed_by == the session) MUST equal env EXPECTED_ASSIGNEE (== BEADS_ACTOR). Fragment line 151.
comparison_invariants.route: bd show `metadata.gc.routed_to` (== camp `assignee` column, the cooked route) MUST equal env EXPECTED_ROUTE (== GC_TEMPLATE/GC_AGENT), enforced only when BOTH are non-empty. Fragment line 156. THIS IS THE ENV-LEG (plan NB1): in the real-fragment §14 gate, env GC_TEMPLATE/GC_AGENT must equal the cooked route or the fragment spins.
comparison_invariants.status: bd show `status` MUST be `open` or `in_progress`. Fragment lines 143-150.
comparison_invariants.id: bd show `id` MUST equal the hook's `bead_id`. Fragment line 138.

reconciliation._comment: Step 4 — cross-check verbs_static against phase-3's served set. Served: gc hook --claim --json, gc runtime drain-ack, gc convoy status --json, bd show/update/close/list/ready/create.
reconciliation.verdict: NO STOP. Both `gc prime` (fragment line 24) and `bd mol` (line 10) appear ONLY in PROHIBITION prose ('Do not run `gc prime`', 'Do not use `bd mol current`'); neither is on ANY executed branch (verified by driving happy/fail_close/config_reject under sh). No phase-4 verb (prime/mail) is required on the claim->close->drain path. `bd list`/`bd ready` also appear only as prohibitions but are in the served set anyway. Refusing `gc prime`/`bd mol` is therefore safe: the fragment never calls them.
